player_utterance: Quiet out here. You picked the good spot.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup in one hand, the other in his coat pocket

player_utterance: no im not family, just a guy who used to know him, I'm not here to make you talk. But I noticed you in there — your mouth didn't move on the amen

believer_state_after.posture: standing still, the cup lowered slightly, looking at the player now rather than past them

player_utterance: Was it the words about him — or the words about you?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting down slowly on the bench, setting the cup beside him
sitting on the bench, hands folded between his knees, looking out at the courtyard

voice_offers.read: He sat down; pressing now scares off what's surfacing.
He didn't deny it. The question landed — name it.
voice_offers.line: You teach this stuff for a living. So which one was it — his soul, or yours?

player_utterance: You knew what kind of man he was. And you sat anyway. That's the weight, isn't it?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, elbows on knees now, looking down at the flagstones

voice_offers.read: Silence worked once; now a small human gesture earns more.
voice_offers.line: Take your time. I'm not going anywhere.

voice_offers.read: He's softened; one quiet question can split the seam.
voice_offers.line: You didn't say amen because you didn't mean it, did you.

voice_offers.read: Pin him to his own discipline before feeling closes again.
voice_offers.line: You teach the catechism. What does it say happens to a man like him?

player_utterance: you reciting of the verses is impressive, what else do you knon

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, elbows on knees, half-turned toward the player

voice_offers.read: He's looking down, not away; let the question land.
He's bending. One more honest word finishes it.
voice_offers.line: You sat there and said the words over him. And part of you was the one being buried.

player_utterance: of course im not testing you, i just like to talk to people who has faith in them, and i like how they connect their scripture and thier life

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, turning slightly more toward the player, hands loosely clasped

voice_offers.read: Seeker fumbled; lost the thread. Recover the pressure.
voice_offers.line: Forget the verses. I'm asking about you. What did you know about him that no one else did?

voice_offers.read: The fumble can be reframed as genuine inquiry into doctrine.
voice_offers.line: You teach this for a living — what does Luther actually say about a man who dies unreconciled?

voice_offers.read: The seeker stumbled; let John have the small ground back.
voice_offers.line: That came out wrong. I meant — you've clearly carried these words a long time.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, reaching into his inside jacket pocket, drawing out the folded funeral program

voice_offers.read: He's turning toward us; warmth is working where pressure failed.
voice_offers.line: What was a verse he loved? Not one you teach — one he carried.

voice_offers.read: The soft approach is a leash; he'll talk himself into the wound.
voice_offers.line: So tell me one. A verse and the life beside it. Start with his.

player_utterance: [SOURCE VOICE INVOKED: Matthew 25 — the least of these. Whole, please.]

voice_offers.read: He's reaching for something he carries; let him show it.
The program is the wound folded up; name it.
voice_offers.line: You've been holding onto that the whole time, haven't you.

player_utterance: I'm right here, John. The verse can take all the time it needs.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, head still in his hands, then slowly lifting it, not looking at the player, looking at the candle

voice_offers.read: He's reciting it like a man holding himself up.
He picked the judgment chapter. Ask why that one.
voice_offers.line: Why that chapter, John. Of everything you could carry — why the one about who gets turned away.

player_utterance: [SOURCE VOICE INVOKED: Luke 16. The rich man and Lazarus. All of it.]

believer_state_after.posture: sitting at first, then sliding off the bench partway through, ending on his knees in the dust of the courtyard with his hands flat against the flagstones

voice_offers.read: He's lifting his head; the candle holds him. Let him find words.

voice_offers.line: Say it, John. The whole verse. Out loud.

voice_offers.line: Lazarus at the gate, John. Who was Lazarus to you.
